title: MatchFixture
description: Canonical competitive-match row. `MatchFixture` is the PHP class name for a match (renamed from `Match` — a PHP 8.0 reserved keyword — while the domain concept, table `match_fixtures`, ULID prefix `mat_`, and every consumer-facing string keep talking about matches). Belongs to Tenant + Season + Branch (required) + Team (nullable — multi-team meets) + Competition (nullable — friendlies) + Facility (nullable — venue TBD) + OpponentLogo (nullable). NEVER carries application_id, region_id, organization_id, or scope_node_id — all cascade through tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §5. State machine: SCHEDULED -> LINEUP_SET -> IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED / POSTPONED / CANCELLED / ABANDONED.

sport_key.description: Sports registry key. Discriminates into the scoring strategy (mechanism #4) so football / swimming / gymnastics all share one match model.

team_id.description: Home team for standard home/away matches. NULL for multi-team events (galas / festivals / swim meets) — participants live on match_participants instead.

competition_id.description: NULL for friendlies. When set, MatchResultPublished triggers a competition-standings recompute.

facility_id.description: The bookable venue. NULL when the venue is TBD or when the branch has a single default pitch.

opponent_name.description: Free-text for standard home/away matches (`Downtown Wanderers U10`). NULL for multi-team events or internal matches — participants describe themselves via match_participants.

opponent_logo_id.description: Cached opponent logo. Reused across matches via normalized-name lookup.

home_or_away.description: Enum: home / away / neutral. NULL for multi-team events (participants each declare their own home/away in match_participants when relevant).

kickoff_at.description: Scheduled kickoff. Rescheduling on status=LINEUP_SET or beyond re-fires lineup-publish gate + notify.

status.description: Enum: SCHEDULED / LINEUP_SET / IN_PROGRESS / COMPLETED / POSTPONED / CANCELLED / ABANDONED.

lineup_published_at.description: Set on POST /lineup/publish; cleared on /lineup/unpublish. Drives the parent-facing published-lineup view + the NotifyMatchLineupPublishedJob dispatch.

result_summary_json.description: Denormalized snapshot of match_results for cheap list-view rendering. Shape depends on sport's scoring strategy — { home_score, away_score, outcome, motm_athlete_id } for score-based; { winning_lane, winning_time_seconds } for time-based; { winning_participant_id, winning_score } for apparatus.

notes_snapshot.description: Rendered public/coach recap excerpt for fast display on the match card. Regenerated from match_notes on write.

x-invariants: team_id IS NOT NULL XOR match has >= 2 rows in match_participants (a standard match has a team; a multi-team meet has participants)
opponent_name IS NULL when the match is multi-team (team_id IS NULL) — opponents live on match_participants
lineup_published_at IS NOT NULL implies status IN ('LINEUP_SET', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED')
status='COMPLETED' implies at least one row in match_results with published_at IS NOT NULL
status='POSTPONED' implies kickoff_at may be updated without re-approval; status='LINEUP_SET' or beyond blocks kickoff_at updates without a reschedule action + re-notify
status='CANCELLED' + status='ABANDONED' are terminal — cannot transition back
sport_key IMMUTABLE post-create — matches never change sport
team_id IMMUTABLE once at least one squad entry exists (locks the participating team)
facility_id changes on status='LINEUP_SET' or beyond fire the venue-change hook + require re-approval + re-notify
